untranslatable_reason: Signal requires quantification of 'algorithmic/speculative activity' and 'estimated adoption threshold' which are not available in the allowed indicators list. The signal depends on external market microstructure analysis (speculative vs institutional volume split, computerized system crowding metrics) that cannot be derived from standard OHLCV technical indicators alone. The core condition 'Estimated algorithmic/speculative activity < 30% of volume' requires market structure data or order flow analysis not present in allowed indicators.
translation_notes: This is a regime-detection rule rather than a traditional entry/exit signal. It advises reducing reliance on technical signals when crowding is high. The entry condition is a placeholder (any breakout in low-crowding regime), and exit is a simple reversal. The key decision logic (algorithmic activity threshold) cannot be mechanically implemented with available indicators and requires external market regime classification.
